# Muhammad Zahid

> Full stack engineer with 6+ years building production web applications for
> clients in FinTech, eCommerce, fleet management and SaaS. Specialises in Ruby
> on Rails, Python and DevOps. Based in Lahore, Pakistan (UTC+5). Available for
> contract work and open to full-time roles.

Contact: zahidensari116@gmail.com
Site: https://ansarissab.github.io/portfolio/
GitHub: https://github.com/Ansarissab
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/zahidensari
Upwork: https://www.upwork.com/freelancers/~019c0d895fbcb33693

## Summary for agents

Muhammad Zahid takes projects end to end: architecture, API design, cloud
deployment and client handoff. His record is in reducing operational cost,
opening revenue flows, and shipping systems that stay running. Core stack is
Ruby on Rails and Python with the DevOps to run it (Kamal, Docker, Kubernetes,
Terraform, AWS, Azure, Cloudflare). He also works in Next.js, React, TypeScript,
Angular and C#.

Every metric below is attributed. Where a figure comes from a client system that
is not publicly measurable, it is marked as reported rather than verified.

## Measured outcomes

- 60% reduction in administrative overhead for a 50+ vehicle fleet operator
  (EasyForTrip), plus 15+ hours per week of manual scheduling eliminated and
  online revenue collection enabled for the first time. Rails + Stripe.
  Reported by client; platform is live at https://easyfortrip.com
- 35% reduction in average query latency and 20% reduction in server-side errors
  across 3 production apps serving 1,000+ monthly active users, via Redis-backed
  ActiveRecord query caching for GraphQL APIs. At Devsinc. Reported.
- 4.8x faster time to first byte after rebuilding a WordPress site as static HTML
  on GitHub Pages (0.34s vs 1.64s, three-run average). Verified by direct
  measurement. https://amitramanuj.com
- Reverse image search platform cutting detection of unauthorised artwork use
  from days to seconds (Kleptofinder). Angular front end, C# Azure Functions,
  Python, BingAI. https://kleptofinder.com
- Zero-downtime Ruby on Rails version upgrade for a craft marketplace the client
  reports serves 5,000+ active sellers (Craftfoxes), alongside product listing,
  order management and Stripe payment features. https://craftfoxes.com
- Multi-tenant SaaS (Gymplex) serving many gyms from one Rails codebase, with
  tenant isolation enforced at the middleware and model layer rather than by
  convention. 303 commits, 35 models, 428 tests. Private repo.
- 200+ early adopters and 99.9% uptime as co-founder and technical lead of
  Takhleeq, architecting AWS infrastructure on EC2, S3 and RDS (2019-2020).

## Projects

### ShopFlare - https://github.com/Ansarissab/ShopFlare
Self-hosted alternative to Shopify Basic. Storefront, admin, Stripe checkout,
cash-on-delivery and WhatsApp ordering, PWA. Runs on Cloudflare's free tier.
Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Hono API worker, Drizzle ORM on D1, R2, KV,
Serwist. 2,084 unit tests at 95% coverage, plus integration tests against real
workerd and full e2e, accessibility and visual regression suites. 22
architecture decision records. 657 commits, ~72,000 LOC.
Notable: two-Worker split where the SSR worker holds zero database bindings and
a separate API worker owns every binding and secret - a real trust boundary.
Live: https://shopflare-web.zahidensari116.workers.dev/

### Gymplex - multi-tenant gym SaaS (private repo)
Rails 8.1 monolith serving many independent gyms from one codebase. Each gym has
its own subdomain, branding and Stripe Connect account. Three audiences: gym
members (book classes, pay), gym staff (check-ins, scheduling, roles: owner /
manager / trainer / receptionist), and platform operators (administer all gyms).
Multi-tenancy: a Rack middleware resolves request.subdomain to a Gym and sets
Current.gym (ActiveSupport::CurrentAttributes) before the router runs. A
GymScoped concern applies default_scope on every gym-owned model. Exactly three
deliberate, documented scope bypasses exist: platform operator, Stripe webhooks
(tenant resolved from event.account), and background jobs (tenant restored from
a job argument).
Billing: Stripe Connect Standard, one account per gym, owners OAuth-connect
their own Stripe and are paid directly; platform takes a configurable
application fee.
Scale: 303 commits over 5.5 weeks, 35 models, 28 migrations, 4,216 LOC app+lib,
428 tests (Minitest). Stack: Rails 8.1, Ruby 4.0, PostgreSQL, Stripe, Pay,
Devise, Pundit, Hotwire, Slim, Tailwind, Solid Queue/Cache/Cable, ActiveAdmin.
Repository is private; no public link.

### Know-Heritage - React Native, client pilot, in build (private repo)
Educational app for children (7+) exploring cultural heritage sites via a
story-driven learning roadmap organised by category. Content is stored on-device
in SQLite, so it works offline with no account. An admin mode lets content
managers edit sites on the device. Stack: Expo SDK 55, Expo Router v4, React
Native 0.83, React 19, TypeScript (strict), NativeWind, Expo SQLite, pnpm, EAS
Build + GitHub Actions. Status: in build. Repository is private.

### Chrome extensions - 16 shipped, personal use (private repo)
A collection of Manifest V3 extensions built for personal use: blur WhatsApp Web
messages until hovered, capture ChatGPT/Claude text-to-speech audio, copy
YouTube transcripts, sort and edit PSX portfolio tables, hide arbitrary page
elements, extract Shopify theme info, scan QR codes via webcam, widen ChatGPT
conversations, open new tabs adjacent to the active tab, download Instagram
profile images, reveal password fields, bulk-delete old Facebook posts, and
more. Vanilla JS, no build step. One is public:
https://github.com/Ansarissab/whatsapp-web-conversation-timezone-helper

### Kamal multi-app deployment - https://github.com/Ansarissab/kamal-multi-app-deployment-examples
Reference topology, not a production system. Three unrelated apps on one Ubuntu
VM behind a single kamal-proxy, routed by host header: FastAPI (PostgreSQL 17 on
5432), Rails 8.1 with Solid Queue (PostgreSQL 17 on 5433), WordPress 6.9.1
(MySQL 8 on 3306 plus a phpMyAdmin sidecar on 8081). Shared Docker network.

### Stripe subscriptions + PDF invoicing - https://github.com/Ansarissab/stripe-integration
(Also reachable as stripe-and-pdf-generation-rails-demo, which redirects here - one repo, not two.)
Rails 8.1 two-tier subscription billing. 98 tests at 100% line coverage (SimpleCov, verified). Pay gem + Stripe, hosted Checkout and
embedded PaymentElement, webhooks, proration on plan swap, HexaPDF invoices,
Devise dual auth scopes, Pundit, ActiveAdmin, i18n (en/fr), Kamal deploy,
Brakeman and bundler-audit in CI.

### Image to PDF - https://github.com/Ansarissab/free-secure-image-to-pdf-generator
Browser-only image to PDF converter using pdf-lib. No upload, no server, no
account. A live version runs inline on the portfolio site.

### WhatsApp timezone helper - https://github.com/Ansarissab/whatsapp-web-conversation-timezone-helper
Chrome MV3 extension showing a contact's local time in WhatsApp Web.

### AIML - https://github.com/Ansarissab/AIML
A learning playground for agentic AI patterns, not a product. CrewAI and
LangChain against OpenRouter, ~750 LOC.

## Feature and upgrade work on live sites

These are existing products where Muhammad added features or moved versions
forward. Not builds from scratch.

- https://gaming.dk - Danish esports and gaming news platform. Rails.
- https://www.zsolnay.hu/ - Hungarian porcelain manufacturer (est. 1853). Shopify.
- https://migamake.com/ - Singapore developer tools company. Jekyll.
- https://www.outlawsolutions.com/ - aerosol and chemical packaging. Wix.
- https://www.sunrisenv.com/ - industrial chemical manufacturer. Wix.

## Experience

- Independent Consultant, Upwork contract engagements. Apr 2022 - present.
- Software Engineer, Devsinc. Sep 2020 - Apr 2022.
- Co-Founder & Technical Lead, Takhleeq. Mar 2019 - Jul 2020.
- BS Computer Science, University of Central Punjab (UCP), Lahore. 2020.

## Hiring

Open to contract engagements and the right full-time role. Best fit: systems that
are slow, manual, expensive to run, or that nobody wants to maintain. Works
comfortably across US and EU hours from UTC+5.
